nodes.label: ECE 3
nodes.title: Introduction to Electrical Engineering
nodes.description: Survey of electrical engineering disciplines, tools, and career paths. Open to EE and Computer Engineering majors.

nodes.label: ECE 5
nodes.title: Introduction to ECE
nodes.description: Exposes freshmen to ECE sub-fields through faculty lectures and weekly Arduino-based project labs.

nodes.label: CMPSC 16
nodes.title: Problem Solving with C++
nodes.description: Programming fundamentals in C++: control flow, functions, arrays, and object-oriented design for engineering applications.

nodes.label: ECE 10A
nodes.title: Circuits & Systems I (+ lab)
nodes.description: Foundations of analog and digital circuits and systems. Core prep for signals, electronics, and logic design tracks.

nodes.label: ECE 10B
nodes.title: Circuits & Systems II (+ lab)
nodes.description: Continuation of circuit analysis: AC circuits, frequency response, and system-level analog/digital concepts.

nodes.label: ECE 10C
nodes.title: Circuits & Systems III (+ lab)
nodes.description: Advanced circuits topics bridging to upper-division EE: transforms, feedback, and design-oriented analysis.

nodes.label: ECE 130A
nodes.title: Signal Analysis & Processing I
nodes.description: Continuous-time signals and systems: Fourier analysis, filtering, and introduction to signal processing methods.

nodes.label: ECE 130B
nodes.title: Signal Analysis & Processing II
nodes.description: Discrete-time signals and systems: sampling, Z-transforms, and digital filter design fundamentals.

nodes.label: ECE 139
nodes.title: Probability & Statistics
nodes.description: Probability models, random variables, and statistical methods for communications, signal processing, and ML.

nodes.label: ECE 134
nodes.title: Introduction to Fields & Waves
nodes.description: Electromagnetic fields, Maxwell's equations, transmission lines, and wave propagation for RF and wireless.

nodes.label: ECE 15A
nodes.title: Fundamentals of Logic Design
nodes.description: Boolean algebra, combinational and sequential logic, and hardware description for digital system design.

nodes.label: ECE 152A
nodes.title: Digital Design Principles
nodes.description: Advanced digital design: finite state machines, timing, and implementation strategies for modern hardware.

nodes.label: ECE 153B
nodes.title: Sensor & Peripheral Interface Design
nodes.description: Embedded interfaces, sensors, and peripherals. Prerequisite to senior project (ECE 188A); ECE 153A is an alternative.

nodes.label: ECE 188A
nodes.title: Senior EE Project I
nodes.description: First quarter of capstone team project: proposal, design specification, and initial implementation.

nodes.label: ECE 188B
nodes.title: Senior EE Project II
nodes.description: Second quarter of capstone: prototype development, testing, and iteration toward final deliverable.

nodes.label: ECE 188C
nodes.title: Senior EE Project III
nodes.description: Final capstone quarter: integration, documentation, and presentation of the completed engineering project.

nodes.label: ENGR 101
nodes.title: Ethics in Engineering
nodes.description: Professional ethics, social responsibility, and case studies in engineering decision-making.

nodes.label: RF Engineer
nodes.title: RF / Wireless Engineer
nodes.description: Design antennas, RF circuits, and wireless communication systems for telecom and aerospace.

nodes.label: Hardware Engineer
nodes.title: Hardware / Digital Design
nodes.description: Board-level design, FPGA/ASIC development, and embedded hardware for consumer and industrial products.

nodes.label: DSP Engineer
nodes.title: Signal Processing Engineer
nodes.description: Develop algorithms for audio, imaging, radar, and communications in software and hardware.
